title: Fair distribution ledger
summary: A simple ledger for shared supplies that reduces arguments and hidden depletion.
content: Shared supplies disappear when distribution is not visible. A ledger records item, quantity, date, receiver group, reason, remaining stock, and next review. It should not humiliate people. Use categories instead of private details when possible. Fairness includes special needs, not equal portions for unequal risk. For Fair distribution ledger, the useful record should include the date, the person responsible, what was checked, what stayed uncertain, and the next safe action. Review fair distribution ledger after weather changes, illness changes, new damage, new supplies, or movement to another place, because small changes can make an old decision unsafe.
materials: Basic hand tools if relevant — for «Fair distribution ledger»
steps: Track remaining stock after each change.
warnings: Do not use the ledger to punish people.
Do not hide shortages until supplies are gone.
common_mistakes: Working from memory only for Fair distribution ledger.
Letting one person control the whole system for Fair distribution ledger.
Skipping records because the task feels simple for Fair distribution ledger.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Fair distribution ledger with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Fair distribution ledger if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Fair distribution ledger and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Fair distribution ledger or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Fair distribution ledger are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores food safety, dry storage, clean tools, pests, and water use and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Fair distribution ledger”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Fair distribution ledger as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Fair distribution ledger when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Fair distribution ledger.
short_term: For the first hours of Fair distribution ledger, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Fair distribution ledger remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Fair distribution ledger fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Fair distribution ledger to the real environment: check food safety, dry storage, clean tools, pests, and water use, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
